title: Fatty acid extraction and derivatisation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"10vmh","text":"This protocol enables direct, one-pot fatty acid extraction and derivatisation of plant and bacterial samples for preparation for GC-MS analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5qlsh","text":"The protocol was established by the Weber lab for plant seedlings and seeds, but was successfully used for cyanobacteria and E.coli as well.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":125,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"8m2nk","text":"Always use clean glass tubes and avoid using washing detergent","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4kplf","text":"acrid, always wear safety goggles and gloves, don’t use plastic","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: samples with 4 oD-units ( e.g.: oD = 1, you will need 4 ml culture ) you should make 4 or more replicates
centrifuge in a clean glass tube @ 4500 x g 00:10:00

200 µl C17 internal standard (1 mg/ml hexane) + 9,8 ml MeOH/3N HCl caution! fill in a beaker and take the needed amount out of this beaker, don't take out directly from original container to avoid contaminating stock solution. always work with gloves
heat @ 90°C after 5 minutes re-tighten lids of the glass tubes! 01:00:00
add 1 ml hexane hexane is very volatile, that's why working quickly is necessary
transfer hexane phase in to GC vial with screw cap
dilute samples ( 10 µl sample + 90 µl hexane ) wash hamilton between samples with hexane all of these samples can be frozen @ -20 until further analysis
add 1 ml extraction solution to samples and blank For each new batch of samples, include a blank
creator: Igem Dusseldorf
